name: fix-a-bug
description: Tight defect-fix loop: reproduce, scope, fix, verify, review. Each stage is a literal maddu invocation so a run lands receipts in the Plans, Loops, Reviews, and Cost cockpit routes.

stages.name: reproduce
stages.intent: Register and claim the lane to reproduce the defect. `maddu register --runtime claude-code --role implementer --focus "reproduce <bug>"`, then `maddu lane claim --lane <lane-id> --focus "reproduce + fix <bug>"`.

stages.name: scope
stages.intent: Declare the slice scope around the suspect area. `maddu slice scope-declare --paths "<dirs>" --focus "isolate root cause of <bug>"`.

stages.name: fix
stages.intent: Drive the fix with an iterative loop. `maddu loop ralph --goal "fix <bug>: edit, run failing test, repeat until green"` so each iteration is recorded in the cockpit Loops route.

stages.name: verify
stages.intent: Confirm the fix and no regressions. Run the failing test, then `maddu test` and `maddu doctor` to surface any FAIL rows.

stages.name: review
stages.intent: Record the fix and close the slice. Stop the slice first — `maddu slice-stop "SLICE STOP: <summary>..."` with targets, paths, gates, and learnings — then `maddu review run --slice <slice-event-id>` to review the slice event.
